targetTruthTable.desc: A=1 kills A̅C, no B for BC or AB
AB + BC: consensus covers the A transition!

hint: Build three AND terms: AB = AND(A,B), A̅C = AND(NOT(A),C), BC = AND(B,C). The consensus term BC covers the hazard when A transitions while B=C=1. Combine: F = OR(OR(AB, A̅C), BC). Four outputs: [AB, A̅C, BC, F].
title: Hazard-Free Consensus Logic
objective: Eliminate a static-1 hazard with a consensus term
description: F = AB + A̅C has a static-1 hazard: when B=C=1 and A transitions 1→0, both products momentarily go LOW, causing a glitch spike. Adding the consensus term BC creates F = AB + A̅C + BC, which is hazard-free because BC bridges the gap. Build all three products and the glitch-free output. Four outputs: [AB, A̅C, BC, F].
